positional_bias.note: max_share is exactly 0.40, not strictly greater than threshold; PASS.

length_bias.note: Independent measurement confirms builder report: longest_correct_count=0.

distractor_plausibility.note: All distractors represent plausible beginner misconceptions grounded in corpus concepts. One score-2 distractor (q3: 'Larger models always cost less because they share more weights') is slightly weak but not absurd.

coverage_map.lo: Define what a parameter (weight) is in a neural network, using a plain-language explanation that a non-technical person could follow.
Explain how parameters encode knowledge — describing what gets compressed into weights during training.
Describe why parameter count (scale) matters for capability, with reference to the concept of scaling laws.
Explain what quantisation is and why the same model can appear at different file sizes without changing its parameter count.
Apply an understanding of parameter count to practical model-selection decisions, such as trading capability for speed or cost.

beginner_fairness_and_scope.note: All stems use plain language; all concepts tested are defined in corpus §5.1–§5.5 or stated prerequisites. q5 distractor mentioning fine-tuning does not require knowledge of fine-tuning to evaluate correctly.

verdict: Pass — all five checks clean. Positional bias exactly at 0.40 limit (not exceeded). Length bias confirmed at 0/5 longest-correct. All 15 distractors plausible. All 5 LOs covered 1:1 with no orphan questions. Quiz is beginner-fair and on-scope for topic 3.5.
